---
name: Bug Report
about: Report a bug in NETBREAKER
title: "[BUG] "
labels: bug
assignees: ''
---

## Describe the Bug

A clear and concise description of what the bug is.

> Example: *Running `nmap -sV -sC 192.168.10.14` does not award recon credits even on first scan.*

---

## NETBREAKER Version

Check the splash screen or the boot message in the terminal (`NETBREAKER OS vX.X`).

- Version: `v5.x`

---

## To Reproduce

Steps to reproduce the behaviour:

1. Open the file in browser
2. Create or load an operative
3. Navigate to `...` / run command `...`
4. Observe the issue

---

## Expected Behaviour

A clear and concise description of what you expected to happen.

> Example: *Credits should increase by 15 per CVE found on first nmap of a target.*

---

## Actual Behaviour

What actually happened instead.

> Example: *No credits were awarded. The message `[*] No exploitable CVEs found` appeared even though the target has known vulnerabilities.*

---

## Pane / Feature Affected

Check all that apply:

- [ ] Terminal — command behaviour or output
- [ ] Browser pane — INTERCEPT / cURL / FUZZER / COOKIES / DIFF
- [ ] Files pane — filesystem navigation or virtual files
- [ ] Network map — rendering, zoom, pan, or node interaction
- [ ] CVE-DB — search, display, or discovery
- [ ] Shop — purchasing, tool availability
- [ ] Save system — save, load, or delete operative
- [ ] Theme — light/dark toggle
- [ ] Economy — credits, REP, or contract rewards
- [ ] Mining — miner deploy or income tick
- [ ] OPSEC — wanted level, clearlogs, rotateip
- [ ] Other: ___

---

## Console Errors

Open your browser's developer console (F12 → Console tab) and paste any red errors here.

```
Paste console errors here, or write "None"
```

---

## Screenshots

If applicable, add screenshots or a screen recording to help explain the problem.

---

## Environment

**Desktop:**

| Field | Value |
|-------|-------|
| OS | e.g. Windows 11, macOS 14, Ubuntu 22.04 |
| Browser | e.g. Chrome 124, Firefox 125, Safari 17 |
| File served from | e.g. local file (`file://`), Claude.ai, GitHub Pages |

**Mobile (if applicable):**

| Field | Value |
|-------|-------|
| Device | e.g. iPhone 15, Samsung Galaxy S23 |
| OS | e.g. iOS 17.4, Android 14 |
| Browser | e.g. Safari, Chrome Mobile |

---

## Save State Context

If the bug involves credits, REP, contracts, or progression — include your operative's current state if relevant:

- Credits: 
- REP: 
- Tools owned: 
- Targets discovered: 
- Current wanted level: 

*(You can find this with the `whoami` command in the terminal.)*

---

## Additional Context

Any other context, workarounds you found, or related issues.
